children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Out of any money in the Treasury of the United States not otherwise appropriated, there are appropriated for making payments to States, Tribal governments, and units of local government under this section, $150,000,000,000 for fiscal year 2020.

children.label: A
children.text: $3,000,000,000 of such amount for making payments to the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa; and

children.label: B
children.text: $8,000,000,000 of such amount for making payments to Tribal governments.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), not later than 30 days after , the Secretary shall pay each State and Tribal government, and each unit of local government that meets the condition described in paragraph (2), the amount determined for the State, Tribal government, or unit of local government, for fiscal year 2020 under subsection (c).March 27, 2020

children.heading: Direct payments to units of local government
children.label: 2 Direct payments to units of local government
children.text: If a unit of local government of a State submits the certification required by subsection (e) for purposes of receiving a direct payment from the Secretary under the authority of this paragraph, the Secretary shall reduce the amount determined for that State by the relative unit of local government population proportion amount described in subsection (c)(5) and pay such amount directly to such unit of local government.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the amount paid under this section for fiscal year 2020 to a State that is 1 of the 50 States shall be the amount equal to the relative population proportion amount determined for the State under paragraph (3) for such fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No State that is 1 of the 50 States shall receive a payment under this section for fiscal year 2020 that is less than $1,250,000,000.

children.heading: Pro rata adjustments
children.label: B Pro rata adjustments
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust on a pro rata basis the amount of the payments for each of the 50 States determined under this subsection without regard to this subparagraph to the extent necessary to comply with the requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount appropriated under paragraph (1) of subsection (a) for fiscal year 2020 that remains after the application of paragraph (2) of that subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: the relative State population proportion (as defined in paragraph (4)).

children.label: A
children.text: the population of the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total population of all States (excluding the District of Columbia and territories specified in subsection (a)(2)(A)).

children.heading: Relative unit of local government population proportion amount
children.label: 5 Relative unit of local government population proportion amount
A
children.text: 45 percent of the amount of the payment determined for the State under this subsection (without regard to this paragraph); and

children.label: i
children.text: the population of the unit of local government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total population of the State in which the unit of local government is located.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount set aside under subsection (a)(2)(A) for such fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: each such District’s and territory’s share of the combined total population of the District of Columbia and all such territories, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Tribal governments
children.label: 7 Tribal governments
children.text: From the amount set aside under subsection (a)(2)(B) for fiscal year 2020, the amount paid under this section for fiscal year 2020 to a Tribal government shall be the amount the Secretary shall determine, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior and Indian Tribes, that is based on increased expenditures of each such Tribal government (or a tribally-owned entity of such Tribal government) relative to aggregate expenditures in fiscal year 2019 by the Tribal government (or tribally-owned entity) and determined in such manner as the Secretary determines appropriate to ensure that all amounts available under subsection (a)(2)(B) for fiscal year 2020 are distributed to Tribal governments.

children.heading: Data
children.label: 8 Data
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the population of States and units of local governments shall be determined based on the most recent year for which data are available from the Bureau of the Census.
are necessary expenditures incurred due to the public health emergency with respect to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID–19);
were not accounted for in the budget most recently approved as of , for the State or government; andMarch 27, 2020

were incurred during the period that begins on , and ends on (or, in the case of costs incurred by a Tribal government, during the period that begins on , and ends on ).March 1, 2020December 31, 2021March 1, 2020December 31, 2022

children.heading: Certification
children.label: e Certification
children.text: In order to receive a payment under this section, a unit of local government shall provide the Secretary with a certification signed by the Chief Executive for the unit of local government that the local government’s proposed uses of the funds are consistent with subsection (d).

children.heading: Oversight authority
children.label: 1 Oversight authority
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall conduct monitoring and oversight of the receipt, disbursement, and use of funds made available under this section.

children.heading: Recoupment
children.label: 2 Recoupment
children.text: If the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury determines that a State, Tribal government, or unit of local government has failed to comply with subsection (d), the amount equal to the amount of funds used in violation of such subsection shall be booked as a debt of such entity owed to the Federal Government. Amounts recovered under this subsection shall be deposited into the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: 3 Appropriation
children.text: Out of any money in the Treasury of the United States not otherwise appropriated, there are appropriated to the Office of the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury, $35,000,000 to carry out oversight and recoupment activities under this subsection. Amounts appropriated under the preceding sentence shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authority of Inspector General
children.label: 4 Authority of Inspector General
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to diminish the authority of any Inspector General, including such authority as provided in the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 1 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given that term in .section 5304(e) of title 25

children.heading: Local government
children.label: 2 Local government
children.text: The term “unit of local government” means a county, municipality, town, township, village, parish, borough, or other unit of general government below the State level with a population that exceeds 500,000.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: State
children.label: 4 State
children.text: The term “State” means the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.

children.heading: Tribal government
children.label: 5 Tribal government
children.text: The term “Tribal government” means the recognized governing body of an Indian Tribe.
